strategy: Team fight (24k, three Lost Shades, Tartarus finale). Assign a hitter; supports blade the hitter and manage traps, healer covers the Lord of Night hits. Hit with Myth, Life, or Death (+30%); never Balance (80% resist).

He counters two ways: attack him WITHOUT a trap on him and he fires his opposite-school natural attack; attack with a single-target spell and he Cleanse Wards off all traps plus his stun block. So always have a trap on him and set up plus hit the same round.
He lays a +40% trap every round — stack your own Feint the same round the hitter fires so the traps get cashed before he can cleanse.
Use DoTs to hold traps if needed — a DoT-only hit won't remove the mark or traps, but avoid single-target and AoE hits until you're committing the kill.
Watch round 4 and every 4th: Lord of Night on a random wizard — keep health topped.
Funnel blades to the hitter and burst him through the setup window.
cheatNotes: Every round he lays a +40% trap (rotating schools) — extra damage if you can hit into them.
He may add an invisible universal-acting trap too; treat his stacked traps as free damage.
Every 4 rounds he interrupt-casts Lord of Night on a random wizard — keep health up.
Trapping him makes him put a Marked Stun Block on himself.
He keeps only one stun block no matter how many traps, and holds it until hit by a non-DoT spell.
A single-target attack makes him Cleanse Ward off all traps and the stun block — set up and hit the same round.
Vaporize can dispel the cleanse cheat.
A DoT-only hit won't remove the mark or traps — useful for keeping traps up.
Attack without a trap on him and he fires his opposite-school natural attack at you — always trap him first.
Hitting with Balance makes him use his Storm natural attack in return.
Multi-hit spells like Minotaur make him fire his natural attack twice.
AoE attacks also trigger the natural-attack counter.
DoT spells trigger the counter only on the initial hit.
Astral spells trigger an unknown-school counter — risky.
Shadow spells trigger an unknown-school counter — risky.
